name: Generative Students Compare Simulated MCQ Behavior With Real Student Responses
statement: Lu and Wang compare GPT-4 simulated student responses with real student item responses and profile-rule behavior, finding useful but uneven alignment across mastered, confused, and unknown rules.
population_or_context: Cambridge International AS and A Level Psychology multiple-choice item evaluation.
time_horizon: arXiv HTML full text retrieved and reviewed on 2026-06-02.
quantitative_note: Table 3 shows simulated percent correct of 60 for mastered rules, 20 for confused rules, and 0 for unknown rules; simulated confusion matching is 0, 80, and 40 respectively, while actual confusion matching is 0, 20, and 40.
caveats: The task is multiple-choice item evaluation rather than constructed-response generation.
The source is not a full validation that simulated students can replace real examinees.
evaluation_method: Profile-response alignment, real-response comparison, item difficulty overlap, and qualitative checks of simulated student behavior.
reported_metrics: Table 3 reports percent correct, percent matching confusion, and most common answer category by profile rule for actual and simulated responses.
limitations: The response format and domain are narrow.
The real-response comparison is preliminary item-evaluation support.

source_locator.section: arXiv HTML method/evaluation sections and Table 3
source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The finding paraphrases the same-item real-response comparison and Table 3 actual-versus-simulated rule alignment values.
